assertions.title: Permission enforcement — restricted page access

steps.action: Create isolated owner/carer accounts, verify them from the shared dev-server email log, and add both to round4-authonboard-20260401-billing-care with roles owner/carer.
steps.expected: A real browser session can sign in as the isolated carer with activeOrgId and role=carer.
steps.observed: Because the real login form did not hydrate, I used the local auth API for setup; /api/auth/session then showed activeOrgId=b650e603-8820-4d0f-9341-bf46f4c47908, role=carer, orgSlug=round4-authonboard-20260401-billing-care.

steps.action: Navigate the carer browser session directly to /round4-authonboard-20260401-billing-care/billing.
steps.expected: 403 Forbidden or an access-denied page for the non-owner role.
steps.observed: The route crashed with the Next.js error overlay and a 500 response instead of a 403/access-denied experience.

steps.expected: 403 Forbidden or an access-denied page for the non-owner role.
steps.observed: This alternate billing route also crashed with a 500 error overlay instead of a 403/access-denied experience.

evidence.consoleErrors: Browser errors reported "Cannot find module './vendor-chunks/tailwind-merge.js'" for both billing routes; console also showed Failed to load resource: 500 (Internal Server Error).

assertions.issues: The implemented billing surfaces do not return 403/access denied for the carer role. Both /[orgSlug]/billing and /[orgSlug]/settings/billing fail with 500 module-load errors instead.

assertions.title: Organisation name validation

steps.action: Sign in as a fresh verified user and open /onboarding step 1.
steps.expected: The onboarding form is interactive and ready to validate organisation-name inputs.
steps.observed: The step 1 UI rendered for round4-authonboard-20260401-fresh@example.test, but the page was only partially interactive because the client surface was not hydrating correctly.

steps.action: Paste an organisation name longer than the 100-character maximum into the Organisation name field.
steps.expected: The app rejects the value with a validation message explaining the max length.
steps.observed: The field silently truncated the value to exactly 100 characters and no #org-name-error message appeared.

steps.action: Clear the Organisation name field and click Continue.
steps.expected: The page shows an inline validation message such as 'Organisation name is required'.
steps.observed: The URL stayed on /onboarding, no org-name error element appeared, and the Continue action did not surface any validation message.

evidence.consoleErrors: On /onboarding, console showed repeated Failed to load resource: 404 errors; no inline validation message was emitted for either case.
evidence.fieldState: Overlong input was truncated to length 100 with maxLength=100 and errorText=null; after clicking Continue with an empty field, location remained http://localhost:3200/onboarding and errorText stayed null.

assertions.issues: The over-maximum organisation name is still silently truncated instead of being rejected with a validation message, and the empty-name path also fails to show the required validation message.

assertions.title: First-visit flow: registration to dashboard

steps.action: Use the real /register page, fill a brand-new account form, and click Create account.
steps.expected: The client submits POST /api/auth/register, then the verified first-run flow can continue toward onboarding.
steps.observed: The register form did not hydrate: the show-password toggle stayed inert, and clicking Create account performed GET /register?name=Round4+Owner&email=... instead of POST /api/auth/register, leaving the browser on the register page.

steps.action: Work around the broken auth UI only for setup, then verify and sign in as a fresh user to reach onboarding.
steps.expected: After verification and login, the user reaches onboarding with no organisation yet.
steps.observed: Using the auth APIs plus the shared dev-server email log, I created/verified round4-authonboard-20260401-fresh@example.test and reached /onboarding; /api/auth/session showed emailVerified=true with memberships=[] and no activeOrgId yet.

steps.action: Attempt to complete onboarding from step 1 to reach the personalised dashboard.
steps.expected: Organisation creation succeeds, the user becomes owner of a free-plan org, activeOrgId is set, and the final dashboard shows domain-appropriate first-visit content.
steps.observed: The onboarding Continue button did not advance the real user flow, the over-max name was silently truncated, and no empty-name validation appeared. Because org creation could not be completed through the user surface, the final dashboard, free-plan UI, owner membership display, and post-onboarding activeOrgId behavior were not reachable.

evidence.consoleErrors: On /register and /login, the show-password toggles stayed inert and console showed repeated Failed to load resource: 404 errors; on /onboarding, console again showed repeated 404 resource errors.
evidence.network: Real register-page submission produced GET /register?name=Round4+Owner&email=round4-authonboard-20260401-owner%40example.test&password=**********%21123&confirmPassword=**********%21123 -> 200 instead of POST /api/auth/register. For the setup workaround, POST /api/auth/register -> 201, GET /api/auth/verify-email?token=... -> 307 to /login?message=email_verified&callbackUrl=%2Fonboarding, POST /api/auth/login -> 200, and GET /onboarding -> 200.
evidence.session: For the fresh verified user, GET /api/auth/session returned emailVerified=true, memberships=[], and no activeOrgId before onboarding completed.

assertions.issues: The first-visit flow cannot complete through the real user surface. Registration and login client controls do not hydrate, the registration form falls back to a GET query submission, onboarding cannot progress to org creation, and the final dashboard/free-plan/owner-membership state is therefore unreachable.

frictions.description: The auth and onboarding client surfaces did not hydrate reliably in this rerun: password-visibility toggles never changed field type, registration fell back to a GET /register?... navigation, and the onboarding Continue button stayed inert.
frictions.resolution: Retried with a fresh browser session and page reloads; when the UI remained broken, I used only the local auth APIs plus the shared dev-server email log to establish isolated sessions and continue observing the real rendered surfaces.

frictions.description: The RBAC assertion required a real carer membership, but the broken onboarding/login UI could not create the isolated org context from the browser alone.
frictions.resolution: Used a one-off Bun/Drizzle script scoped to the round4-authonboard-20260401 namespace to create the isolated org and owner/carer memberships, then verified the resulting billing routes in the browser.

blockers.description: The dev server is currently emitting React Client Manifest/runtime issues (including missing SegmentViewNode and missing vendor chunk modules), which leaves register/login/onboarding controls non-hydrated and crashes dashboard/billing routes.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried the flows with fresh page loads and a fresh browser session; the hydration failures and 500 module-load errors persisted.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 0 passed, 3 failed. VAL-AUTH-017 failed because both billing surfaces returned 500 module-load errors for a carer instead of 403/access denied. VAL-TENANT-002 failed because overlong org names are still silently truncated and empty names show no validation message. VAL-CROSS-001 failed because the real first-visit UI cannot complete registration/onboarding, so the final domain-appropriate dashboard and observable free-plan/owner state are not reachable.
